author: Myriad
text:
Test git helpers: scrub GIT_* via shared module

A cargo test launched from the pre-commit hook inherits GIT_DIR, GIT_INDEX_FILE, GIT_WORK_TREE & friends. Those override current_dir, so an unscrubbed git init/add/commit against a tempdir fixture retargets the host repo instead — corrupting the live worktree mid-delivery (root-caused in bl-0151).

The hook itself was already hardened to unset the git-local env (bl-38c5); this hardens the test harness as the second layer. New src/git_test_support.rs exposes git_run / git_stdout built on git::clean_git_command, so the scrub set is defined exactly once and shared with the production git path.

Every unit-test git helper now routes through it, replacing the duplicated per-file scrub-and-run copies; confirmed culprits delivery_test_support, state_repo_test_support, delivery_remote_tests and human_gate_tests are fixed alongside the already-scrubbing files. tests/common rebuilds git/git_ok on clean_git_command and re-exports the now-pub git::GIT_ENV_VARS; the master_url integration tests drop their raw Command::new(git) calls.
